methods.description: Process for preparing sediment samples and imaging otoliths for morphological analysis.

steps.description: Sediment samples were treated 24 h with 10% hydrogen peroxide, washed and sieved (mesh sizes 1 mm, 0.5 mm, 0.25 mm, 0.125 mm, and 0.063 mm), dried, and hand-picked under a stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: treat and sieve sediment samples with hydrogen peroxide and sort under microscope

steps.description: All otoliths were studied with a reflected-light microscope.
Photographs were taken remotely controlled from a computer with a Canon EOS 1000 mounted on the phototube of a Wild M400 photomacroscope at regular field-of-depth levels for each view.
steps.searchable_summary: capture photographs of otoliths using Canon EOS 1000 on photomacroscope

steps.description: The individual photographs of each view were stacked using Helicon Soft's Helicon Focus software.
steps.searchable_summary: stack otolith photographs using Helicon Focus software

steps.description: The continuously focused pictures were digitally processed with Adobe Photoshop to enhance contrast, or balance exposition, or retouch small inconsistencies, such as sand grains, incrustations, or pigmentation spots, insofar as doing so without altering the otolith morphology was possible.
steps.searchable_summary: process otolith images in Adobe Photoshop for contrast and exposure

steps.description: Particular care was taken to photograph inner and outer otolith faces with the central portion of the faces positioned at level to avoid the distortions that can otherwise occur in strongly bent specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph otolith faces to avoid distortion

methods.description: Procedure for measuring otolith dimensions using a camera lucida or photographs.

steps.description: Measurements were taken either with the help of a camera lucida mounted on the microscope or from photographs obtained from the photomacroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: measure otolith dimensions using camera lucida or photographs
